Turn 4 ‚ÄĒ the degree-6 candidate dies; residual tail cleared through degree 7 I used only the three attachments in this message. The problem is still the over-G isomorphism question for the two quotients őďS,őďH defined by rS,rH, with canonical maps to G=‚ü®ŌÉ,ŌĄ‚ą£ŌĄŌÉ=ŌĄ2‚ü©.The prior turns reduced all surviving obstruction tests to the residual Ef-piece, then to the completed mod-2, s=1 Magnus algebra with section twist included.

Turn 3 ended with the possible degree-6 tailőĒ6=ABBABB+ABBBBA+BBAABB+BBABBA,but explicitly warned that this was not yet invariant under the full restricted correction complex.

The result of this turn is:The¬†displayed¬†degree-6¬†tail¬†is¬†not¬†an¬†obstruction.In fact, after allowing the full degree-4 restricted/Bockstein freedom, the residual congruence can be improved toS(Y0,Y1,q)‚Č°X2 (modI8),so the residual obstruction is cleared through degree 7.

Here X=1+A, Y=1+B, I=(A,B), andS(Y0,Y1,q)=(Y1,Y1q)‚ąí1Y1‚ąí2Y03Y0q,the same residual source expression from Turn 3.The structural mistake to avoid is treating a degree-4 correction as a naked associative polynomial 1+W4. In the pro-2 Magnus algebra, actual group elements have forced Bockstein tails. For example, with

C=(X,Y)=X‚ąí1Y‚ąí1XY,the element C2 has initial degree (AB+BA)2 but also a nonzero degree-5 tail. That tail is exactly the kind of term that can change the apparent degree-6 obstruction. So the right complex is built from actual restricted group representatives, not merely their degree-4 initial forms.A compact degree-6 clearing witness is as follows. Define őĪ:=(X,(X,C)),ő≤:=(Y,(X,C)),ő≥:=(Y,(Y,C)).

Their degree-4 initial forms arein4 (őĪ)=AAAB+AABA+ABAA+BAAA,in4 (ő≤)=AABB+BBAA,in4 (ő≥)=ABBB+BABB+BBAB+BBBA.

Together with X4, C2, and Y4, these are the needed restricted degree-4 directions. Starting from Turn 3‚Äôs residual liftY0=YC,Y1=CX‚ąí1Y2,q=Y,replace it byY0(6):=YCX4C2ő≥,Y1(6):=CX‚ąí1Y2őĪő≤,q(6):=YőĪ.

A direct mod-2 Magnus expansion givesS(Y0(6),Y1(6),q(6))=X2+O(I7).So the degree-5 and degree-6 homogeneous parts both vanish. In particular, the old

ABBABB+ABBBBA+BBAABB+BBABBA is a byproduct of one narrow degree-5 clearing choice, not an invariant obstruction.There is a stronger through-degree-7 witness. This is a new choice, not an accumulation of the previous one. Define degree-5 restricted representativesőī0:=(X,őĪ),őī2:=(X,ő≤),őī3:=(Y,C2),őī4:=(Y,ő≤),őī5:=(Y,ő≥).

Then takeY0(7):=YCő≤ő≥őī0őī3őī5,Y1(7):=CX‚ąí1Y2X4C2őī0őī2őī4,q(7):=YC2ő≤Y4őī2.

Substitution givesS(Y0(7),Y1(7),q(7))=X2+O(I8).

Equivalently, after the H1 -transport and section twist already isolated in Turns 1‚Äď3, the residual mod-2, s=1 obstruction vanishes through homogeneous degree 7. This strengthens Turn 3‚Äôs S‚Č°X2 (modI6) to

S‚Č°X2 (modI8).

This still does not prove őďS‚ČÖGőďH. The new structural lesson is that degree 8 is the first layer where one must account for genuine quadratic interactions among lower corrections, because degree 4+4=8. Up through degree 7, the correction problem is essentially linear once the forced Bockstein tails of actual pro-2 representatives are included. At degree 8, a purely linear restricted-Lie image test is no longer sufficient.

ESTABLISHED: The candidate degree-6 residual tail from Turn 3 does not survive the full restricted/Bockstein correction complex. More strongly, there are explicit residual section-twisted lifts withS(Y0,Y1,q)‚Č°X2 (modI8).

GAP: No full over-G isomorphism has yet been constructed. The residual Magnus tower remains uncleared from degree 8 onward, where lower-degree correction interactions first enter nonlinearly.

NEXT: Build the degree-8 obstruction equation as a nonlinear restricted Maurer‚ÄďCartan step, including the quadratic interaction of degree-4 corrections, rather than testing only the degree-7 linear image.[DEG6-DIES-DEG7-CLEARED]
